prabhat-ranjan.name: Prof. Prabhat Ranjan
prabhat-ranjan.tagline: Nuclear fusion scientist, futurist, educator and innovator with nearly two decades of contribution to fusion research at Indian and US national labs.
highlights: Executive Director (CEO), Technology Information, Forecasting & Assessment Council (TIFAC) — India's Technology Think Tank — 2013–2018; led the development of Technology Vision 2035, launched by the Hon'ble Prime Minister
Founding Vice Chancellor, D. Y. Patil International University, Pune (2018–2025); currently Chairman (Research, Innovation and International Relations), Dr. D. Y. Patil Pratishthan, Kolhapur

Nearly two decades of contribution to nuclear fusion research at Saha Institute of Nuclear Physics, Lawrence Berkeley Laboratory, and Institute for Plasma Research (IPR) — Project Leader on Aditya and SST-1 Tokamak reactors
Designed the Lunar Surface and Subsurface Explorer (LuSSeX) for India's Chandrayaan-II mission
Vice Chair of the Council, International Institute for Applied Systems Analysis (IIASA), Vienna (2015–2017)
Chairman, AICTE IDEA Lab National Steering Committee (2020); represents India at ISO JTC/SC43 on Brain–Computer Interface standardisation

Recognition includes Maharashtra Ratna Gaurav Puraskar (2024), listed among 100 Great IITians – Dedicated to the Service of the Nation (2024), Rani Lakshmi Bai Nari Shakti Puraskar from the President of India (2015), and the National Science Talent Search Award (1976)
education: PhD (Physics), University of California, Berkeley — 1986
M.Sc. (Physics), Delhi University — 1981
B.Sc. (Physics), IIT Kharagpur — 1979
Secondary, Netarhat School, Netarhat — 1975 (Bihar state topper, Netarhat School Admission Test, 1970)

abhay-k-jha.name: Abhay K. Jha
abhay-k-jha.tagline: Founder and Managing Director of ESSI Integrated Technologies, with over two decades delivering integrated security and surveillance programmes for India's most sensitive institutions.
highlights: Founder and Managing Director, ESSI Integrated Technologies (2001–present) — systems integration for security, surveillance, smart-city, and transportation infrastructure

Project portfolio includes Parliament House; Prime Minister's Residence and Office; BARC; DRDO and its Advanced Systems Laboratory; National Defence College; National Police Academy; Reserve Bank of India; Delhi Secretariat; IOCL; Airport Authority of India
Current engagements include UT-wide surveillance in Jammu & Kashmir, RBI headquarters and branch deployments, Railway works at Badnera, and ongoing Ministry of Defence and IOCL programmes
A decade at sea (1985–1995) with Great Eastern Shipping (Bombay), East Asiatic Company (Copenhagen), and Teekay Shipping (Vancouver), culminating in a Chief Engineer's Licence
education: Marine Engineering, Marine Engineering Research Institute, Calcutta — First Class — All-India JEE topper for Marine Engineering, 1985
Chief Engineer's Licence (equivalent in rigour to an M.Tech)
Secondary, Netarhat Vidyalaya — Bihar state Class 10 top-20

abhinav-jha.name: Abhinav Jha
abhinav-jha.tagline: Operations leader pairing systems-integration delivery experience with research exposure at Stanford's Intelligent Systems Lab and UCLA's Condensed Matter Physics Lab.
highlights: Co-founder and Operations Director, ASPL Fusion — owns phased programme execution, milestone and budget governance, and strategic partnerships
Director, ESSI Integrated Technologies — delivery of multi-million-dollar systems-integration programmes across R&D, technology sales, delivery, and design
Cross-disciplinary leadership across civil and electrical engineering, networking and telecom, software, premises infrastructure, and critical control-centre operations
Established executive-level engagement with Government leadership for mission-critical programmes
Research exposure at Stanford's Intelligent Systems Lab and UCLA's Condensed Matter Physics Lab
education: Research exposure: Stanford University (Intelligent Systems Lab)
Research exposure: UCLA (Condensed Matter Physics Lab)

varun-vijay-savadi.name: Dr. Varun Vijay Savadi
varun-vijay-savadi.tagline: Post-doctoral neutronics researcher with five-plus years across academia, research, and industry — Monte Carlo simulation, experimental nuclear physics, and accelerator-based tritium-titanium target studies.
highlights: Post-Doctoral Research Fellow, Institute for Plasma Research, Gandhinagar (2023–2025) — Optimisation of tritium losses in solid tritium-titride targets generated through D-T reaction in the 14 MeV neutron generator facility
Executed a DST-SERB funded project (INR 16 Lakhs) during PhD studies
Lead/first author on six peer-reviewed papers covering tritium-target degradation (Physica Scripta 2025), alpha-particle irradiation, thin-layer activation analysis, and 14 MeV neutron reactions
Trained at the World Institute of Nuclear Security Academy (Texas A&M, ORNL); nuclear-security design vulnerability assessment; IUAC School on Nuclear Reactions (2019)
Simulation toolchain: MCNP, PACE4, Cascade, SDTrimSP, SRIM/TRIM, Geant4, ANSYS, SolidWorks
education: PhD (Engineering Sciences), University of Petroleum & Energy Studies, Dehradun — 2019–2023
M.Tech. (Nuclear Science & Technology), University of Petroleum & Energy Studies, Dehradun — 2013–2015
B.E. (Mechanical Engineering), Visvesvaraya Technological University, Belagavi — 2007–2011

nilormi-das.name: Nilormi Das
nilormi-das.tagline: Nuclear scientist with project experience across the IAEA, ITER, IRFM-CEA, IPR and IUAC — focused on fusion neutronics, nuclear safety, and the institutional questions around deploying fusion plants.
highlights: Marie Skłodowska-Curie Fellow (FUSION-EP) — European Master of Science in Nuclear Fusion and Engineering Physics

Intern, International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), Vienna (2023–2024) — key participant in the INPRO Fusion Study; contributed to the IAEA Nuclear Energy Series publication on Legal and institutional issues of prospective deployment of fusion facilities
Master's thesis at IRFM-CEA Cadarache and Aix-Marseille University: neutronics analysis of a magnetic fusion reactor using OpenMC; site visits to ITER and the WEST tokamak
Selected for the 2-week International School on Nuclear Security at IAEA Vienna (2023)
Executive Committee Member, Women in Nuclear (WiN) IAEA Chapter (2024–present)
Published in American Nuclear Society Transactions (2024) and the METO Student Journal of WMD Disarmament (2025); poster at ICTP-IAEA INPRO School (2024)
education: M.Sc. (FUSION-EP), University of Stuttgart and Aix-Marseille University — 2024
Dual B.Tech + M.Tech (Nuclear Science and Technology), Amity Institute of Nuclear Science and Technology, Noida — 2017
6-month Mentorship Programme, Black Sea Women in Nuclear Network (BSWN) — 2025

chandresh-shah.name: Chandresh Shah
chandresh-shah.tagline: R&D and innovation engineer with nine-plus years across nuclear fusion devices, robotics, medical-device development, and digital fabrication.
highlights: Founder, Shreeji Engineering & Consultancy (2024–present) — official vendor for Tata Gotion Green Energy; battery management testing systems for 1–120 cell configurations; medical-device firmware and hardware (cervical-cancer detection, endoscopic injection pumps)

Innovation Officer, D. Y. Patil International University, Pune (2022–2024) — mentored 800+ students in design thinking and embedded systems; built a 10-foot 3D holographic display; created a 3D bio-printer using bio-inks
Scientist / R&D Engineer, Albot Technologies (2021–2022) — IEC device design; oxygen-concentrator design and fabrication during COVID-19; 3D-printed tokamak study model

Earlier research at Institute for Plasma Research as PSSI Scholar (2017–2018) and Academic Project (2019) — Inertial Electrostatic Fusion (IEC) confinement device, Langmuir probe design, high-voltage power-supply development
Built a 40 kV DC power supply, ultra-high-vacuum chamber, and electrostatically-confined plasma device that achieved neutron yield from deuterium
education: B.E. (Automobile Engineering), LD Engineering College, Ahmedabad — 2019
Diploma (Automobile Engineering), LJ Polytechnic, Ahmedabad — 2016

yc-saxena.name: Prof. Y. C. Saxena
yc-saxena.tagline: Founder member of the Institute for Plasma Research; pioneer of experimental plasma physics at PRL Ahmedabad.
highlights: Senior Professor (Retd.), Institute for Plasma Research (IPR), Gandhinagar — one of the founder members of IPR
Faculty at Physical Research Laboratory (PRL), Ahmedabad — instrumental in starting experimental plasma physics research at PRL
Member, past President and Secretary of the Plasma Science Society of India
Fellow, Gujarat Science Academy
Past General Secretary, Asian Plasma & Fusion Association
education: PhD (Cosmic Rays), under the supervision of Dr. Bibha Chowdhuri

srinivasan-ganesan.name: Prof. Srinivasan Ganesan
srinivasan-ganesan.tagline: Ex-Raja Ramanna Fellow (DAE), former IAEA Vienna nuclear physicist, and creator of FENDL-1 — the Fusion Evaluated Nuclear Data file for ITER applications.
highlights: Ex-Raja Ramanna Fellow (Honorary), Department of Atomic Energy
Ex-Scientific Consultant (Honorary), Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser to the Prime Minister, Government of India (2012–2017)
Former Staff Member, Nuclear Physicist, International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), Vienna (1990–1994)
Creator of FENDL-1, the Fusion Evaluated Nuclear Data file for ITER applications — foundational reference for fusion neutronics worldwide
education: Doctoral research in nuclear physics; specialist career in evaluated nuclear data

cvs-rao.name: Prof. C. V. Srinivasa Rao
cvs-rao.tagline: Founder of fusion neutronics research at IPR; 35 years at the Institute for Plasma Research and specialist member of the ITER Governing Council's Scientific & Technical Advisory Committee.
highlights: Scientist SG (Retd.), Institute for Plasma Research (IPR), Gandhinagar — 35 years (1982–2017); Head, Plasma Diagnostics Group; Incharge, Fusion Neutronics Laboratory
Senior Professor, DAE–Homi Bhabha National Institute
Initiated Fusion Neutronics activities at IPR in 2006, following India's full partnership in the ITER Organization
Specialist Member, Scientific & Technical Advisory Committee (STAC) to the ITER Governing Council (2011–2015)
Task force leader for Indian participation in Nuclear Technologies for Fusion Reactors (NTFR)
Developed Soft X-ray Tomography and Hard X-ray diagnostic systems for the Aditya tokamak
Currently Visiting Professor, Institute of Natural Sciences, Adichunchanagiri University, Mysore (2018–present)
education: Post-Doctoral Fellow, IIT Kanpur — Neutron-Induced Reactions using the Van-de Graaff Accelerator (1978–1982)
PhD, Swamy Jnananda Laboratories for Nuclear Research, Andhra University — Nuclear Reactions induced by 14 MeV Neutrons (1973–1978)
